title: Quiz: Molecular Applications of VQE
description: Test your understanding of applying VQE to molecular systems

questions.question: What is a potential energy surface (PES)?

options.text: A plot of kinetic energy vs time
A surface showing how molecular energy varies with nuclear coordinates
The surface of an electrode in a battery

questions.explanation: A potential energy surface shows how the electronic energy of a molecule changes as a function of nuclear coordinates (bond lengths, angles). It reveals equilibrium geometries, transition states, and dissociation pathways.

questions.question: Why does VQE correctly describe H₂ dissociation while Hartree-Fock fails?

options.text: VQE can represent multi-reference character and electron correlation
VQE runs on a faster computer

questions.explanation: At dissociation, H₂ requires a multi-reference description (superposition of |1100⟩ and |0011⟩). VQE can represent this through its parameterized ansatz, while single-reference HF is restricted to a single determinant.

questions.question: What does CAS(6,5) notation mean?
questions.explanation: CAS(n,m) notation means a Complete Active Space with n electrons distributed among m orbitals. CAS(6,5) has 6 electrons in 5 orbitals, requiring 10 spin-orbitals = 10 qubits.

questions.question: How is the bond dissociation energy (BDE) calculated?
questions.explanation: BDE = E(A) + E(B) - E(A-B), representing the energy required to break bond A-B into fragments A and B. A positive BDE means energy is required (bond is stable).

questions.question: Active space methods reduce qubit requirements by only treating a subset of orbitals quantum mechanically.
questions.explanation: True. Active space methods freeze core and high-lying virtual orbitals, only treating a selected set of 'active' orbitals with VQE. This dramatically reduces qubit count while capturing the most important correlation effects.

questions.question: Why is N₂ considered a challenging molecule for quantum chemistry?

options.text: The triple bond shows strong static correlation, especially at dissociation

questions.explanation: N₂ has a triple bond (σ + 2π) that creates strong static correlation effects, particularly during dissociation. This multi-reference character makes it a benchmark for correlated methods like VQE.

questions.question: What is an isodesmic reaction and why is it useful for VQE?

options.text: A reaction with the same bond types on both sides, allowing error cancellation
A reaction with no activation barrier
A reaction involving only hydrogen atoms

questions.explanation: Isodesmic reactions have the same types of bonds on both sides of the reaction. Systematic errors in describing specific bond types tend to cancel, making reaction energies more accurate than absolute energies.

questions.question: Which properties can be computed from a VQE wavefunction? (Select all)
questions.explanation: VQE wavefunctions can compute expectation values like dipole moment (⟨μ̂⟩), spin (⟨Ŝ²⟩), and electron density (⟨n̂(r)⟩). Reaction rate constants require dynamics simulations beyond static VQE.
